jokes.text: Now that the original Michael's back, I suppose we don't need two hands-on-our-hips, pipe-up-our-rectum scolds.
jokes.setup: Michael returns to find family dynamics have shifted
jokes.punchline: Lucille's characteristically cruel dismissal with vivid imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - crude, dismissive, and unnecessarily graphic. Great character integration.

jokes.text: so cruelly served to me from the back seat of my car by someone who thought I was a Lyft driver. Could have been the pink mustache.
jokes.setup: Tobias received divorce papers in an unusual way
jokes.punchline: He was mistaken for a rideshare driver due to his appearance
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor that fits Tobias's obliviousness and the show's visual comedy style.

jokes.text: I dyed it what I assumed was the color of Michael's skin. Although now that you're back, I do wish I had used a less permanent dye.
jokes.setup: Tobias dyed his beard to match Michael's skin tone
jokes.punchline: He regrets the permanence now that he can see the actual color
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Tobias's misguided attempts and poor decision-making. The visual absurdity works well.

jokes.text: His beard tickled me.
jokes.setup: Discussing Tony Wonder's W-shaped beard
jokes.punchline: Tobias's unintentionally suggestive phrasing about physical contact
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias double entendre delivered with complete innocence. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I want to be part of the problem.
jokes.setup: Lindsay explains why she's running for office
jokes.punchline: She admits wanting to make things worse, not better
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of political rhetoric. Perfect economy and character truth.

jokes.text: A puta.
jokes.setup: Lindsay wondering why Lucille is jealous of her
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s blunt, inappropriate Spanish insult
jokes.explanation: Sharp timing and maximum economy. George Sr.'s casual cruelty delivered perfectly.

jokes.text: I hate everything about my life, including you.
jokes.setup: George Sr. and GOB in Mexico dealing with their relationship
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest confession of hatred
jokes.explanation: Dark but effective. Shows the dysfunction while getting a laugh from the bluntness.

jokes.text: Well, that's not the horniest thing I've heard. Daddy not horny.
jokes.setup: George Sr. expressing hatred
jokes.punchline: GOB's misunderstanding leads to awkward third-person reference
jokes.explanation: GOB's sexual misinterpretation and weird self-reference creates uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: I feel like one of these bulls on my breasts.
jokes.setup: George Sr. having an emotional breakdown
jokes.punchline: References the bull imagery on his shirt in a vulnerable moment
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback to his shirt while deepening the metaphor. Typical AD layering.

jokes.text: You're a bull who wants to stick it in everybody, and everybody ends up sticking it in you.
jokes.setup: Extended bull/matador metaphor about life
jokes.punchline: Crude but profound summation of masculine futility
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly profound moment delivered through crude metaphor. Great character depth.

jokes.text: I tried to buy those velvet pants. But I chickened out and got these shirts instead.
jokes.setup: GOB opening up emotionally
jokes.punchline: His vulnerability about fashion choices and cowardice
jokes.explanation: Perfect GOB moment - shallow concerns treated as deep emotional issues.

jokes.text: He had a face like mine... but same.
jokes.setup: GOB trying to describe Tony Wonder
jokes.punchline: His linguistic fumbling makes no sense
jokes.explanation: GOB's inability to articulate thoughts clearly, even about someone he cares about.

jokes.text: Hello, darkness. Sorry, I'm taken.
jokes.setup: GOB hearing a song lyric
jokes.punchline: He thinks someone is addressing him romantically
jokes.explanation: Classic GOB narcissism - assumes everything is about him, even song lyrics.

jokes.text: My God. Must be nice to be wanted, even if it's just your mom. Oh, cry me a blizzard, snowflake.
jokes.setup: George Sr. expressing envy over GOB being wanted
jokes.punchline: GOB's dismissive response with weather-themed twist on common phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits GOB's dismissive personality. Good phrase variation.

jokes.text: Hey. It is hard to resist. Taking advantage of the vulnerable.
jokes.setup: Narrator mentions people taking advantage of vulnerable
jokes.punchline: Maeby directly confirms this while doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where character acknowledges narrator while proving the point. Great structure.

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh. I didn't even recognize you. It's a teenager thing. I could never pull that off. They want to look old, we want to look... You don't look very young.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to compliment Maeby on her appearance
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insults her while trying to be relatable
jokes.explanation: Typical Lindsay foot-in-mouth moment. Shows her self-absorption and poor social skills.

jokes.text: Family of the Month. Year?! Are you for... Mother Family of the Mother Year! Of the year.
jokes.setup: Confusion over what family award they're receiving
jokes.punchline: Increasingly nonsensical award titles
jokes.explanation: Shows the family's confusion and desperation for recognition through escalating nonsense.

jokes.text: which is just tits, teeth and ass, right? Now she's the host of O.C. Can You Say, which is tits, teeth, public affairs, ass, some light interviews. I mean, it's so much more responsibility!
jokes.setup: GOB describing his girlfriend's career progression
jokes.punchline: He adds 'public affairs' to the crude list as if it's meaningful progress
jokes.explanation: Perfect GOB - crude but genuinely proud, missing the point entirely about meaningful work.

jokes.text: She's my Beard now.
jokes.setup: Talking about his girlfriend who was married to John Beard
jokes.punchline: Unintentional double meaning of 'beard' as fake girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Excellent double entendre that GOB doesn't realize. Perfect for his character and the show's layered humor.

jokes.text: Well, technically, it's from the Bluth-Austero Company. Oh, I see. So you're giving it to yourself.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what organization is giving them the award
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're giving themselves an award
jokes.explanation: Great straight-man moment from Michael exposing the family's self-congratulatory nature.

jokes.text: I've never been less ashamed of her. Did I say what I think I said?
jokes.setup: Lucille trying to compliment Lindsay
jokes.punchline: Realizes she delivered a backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - even when trying to be nice, she's still cruel. Great self-awareness moment.

jokes.text: It was during the 1992 African-American riots, and they had every right. Every right. Every right.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they didn't want crew in their house
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate and tone-deaf reference to LA riots
jokes.explanation: Typical Bluth casual racism delivered with complete obliviousness. Dark but characteristic.

jokes.text: They're like two geodes! You can feel them. I can't. My go-to joke is that these are the best two things John Beard's money can buy.
jokes.setup: GOB praising his girlfriend's enhanced breasts
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about their artificiality and his 'go-to joke'
jokes.explanation: GOB's inappropriate oversharing and crude objectification, delivered with pride.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I'm probably grumpy because I was out late last night, raping and murdering. Not cool. I wouldn't joke about that. I rape and murder, and I joke.
jokes.setup: George Michael apologizing for being grumpy
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions serious crimes then doubles down
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark humor from usually innocent George Michael. Shows his character development.

jokes.text: Total regression!
jokes.setup: George Michael returning to childhood behaviors
jokes.punchline: Narrator's clinical diagnosis of obvious behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic narrator moment - stating the obvious with mock authority. Good economy.

jokes.text: God, it's so much smaller than I remember.
jokes.setup: George Michael returning to childhood home
jokes.punchline: Classic adult-returning-to-childhood-space observation
jokes.explanation: Predictable but necessary observation. Gets the job done but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: For scenario two, you just found out that your Darth Vader figure is dating your girlfriend, and you find out he knew she was your... Whatever. Who gives a shit?
jokes.setup: George Michael teaching through action figures
jokes.punchline: Breaks character to express real frustration about his situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where George Michael processes his real trauma through play therapy. Great character work.

jokes.text: Well, truthfully... No, I'll just tell you the truth: it's a wig.
jokes.setup: George Michael asking about her hair
jokes.punchline: Maeby's redundant preamble to a simple admission
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Maeby's overthinking. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Everything I've given her to do or say has only made her go up in the polls. It's like the more offensive it is, the more people get behind her.
jokes.setup: Maeby is trying to sabotage Lindsay's campaign
jokes.punchline: Her sabotage attempts are making Lindsay more popular
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire that feels prescient. Good setup for the absurdist campaign storyline.

jokes.text: This so-called reporter from the Wee Guardian says I was right. Now, suddenly, she's 'Perhaps I've recalled incorrectly. I'm positively knackered.'
jokes.setup: Lindsay mocking a reporter with speech problems
jokes.punchline: Her terrible attempt at British accent and phrases
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's cruel nature and poor impression skills. Character-appropriate cruelty.

jokes.text: So the kissing is part of a plan? Yeah. I thought we could do it when the whole family is on camera getting the Family of the Year Award.
jokes.setup: George Michael thinking Maeby wants to kiss him romantically
jokes.punchline: She reveals it's just a publicity stunt
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment highlighting their dysfunctional relationship dynamic.

jokes.text: And then, I open my mouth, and I have these rotted out meth teeth. And I'm like, 'Then why do I gotta my cousin for some meth money, Mom?'
jokes.setup: Maeby describing her plan for mother to praise her
jokes.punchline: Reveals shocking twist involving drugs and family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Darkly creative and shows Maeby's twisted thinking. Good shock value that fits character.

jokes.text: We forget, but we never forgive.
jokes.setup: Explaining the family motto
jokes.punchline: Backwards version of normal saying reveals family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion of common wisdom that perfectly captures Bluth family psychology. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Although, by not forgiving, you're throwing everything out and just keeping the hate. Well, how do you think she keeps that figure?
jokes.setup: Michael pointing out the flaw in their family logic
jokes.punchline: George Sr. suggests Lucille's hatred keeps her thin
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever observation about Lucille's personality. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: When I was a kid, you said, 'You want a trophy? Try harder, G.O.B.' But I was like, 'But you gave one to everybody else. What happened to everybody gets a trophy?'
jokes.setup: GOB recounting childhood trophy disappointment
jokes.punchline: He wanted participation trophies that he now mocks
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing GOB's childhood trauma and hypocrisy about participation culture.

jokes.text: And you're gonna be surprised to hear this coming from me... May be a little bit too hard.
jokes.setup: GOB continuing to discuss his girlfriend's enhanced breasts
jokes.punchline: Admits even he finds them unnaturally hard
jokes.explanation: More GOB oversharing. The setup acknowledging his usual preferences makes it work.

jokes.text: Everyone gets a trophy. Count me out. Fine. Everyone but G.O.B. No!
jokes.setup: Family deciding on trophy distribution
jokes.punchline: GOB's immediate reversal when excluded
jokes.explanation: Perfect GOB - principles only matter until they personally affect him. Great economy.

jokes.text: That is not Buster.
jokes.setup: Michael noticing something wrong with family member
jokes.punchline: Reveals someone is impersonating Buster
jokes.explanation: Good reveal moment that pays off setup. Works well with show's complex plotting.

jokes.text: I don't really want to be an actor. It's not what I want to do.
jokes.setup: Actor hired to play Buster
jokes.punchline: Breaks character to complain about acting
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about acting profession delivered by someone literally acting.

jokes.text: I'm going in and out of Michael like a cuckoo clock bird.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining his confusion about playing Michael
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor comparing himself to clock mechanism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias - overly dramatic and bizarre metaphor for simple concept. Great character voice.

jokes.text: I mean, that guy obviously has a giant rubber hand, and I have a giant melted hand that smells like Bounce.
jokes.setup: Buster distinguishing himself from someone in a video
jokes.punchline: Specific details about his prosthetic hand's smell
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific Buster detail that's both absurd and somehow logical for him.

jokes.text: you know, which is... that's the universal sign for 'things are over.'
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his breakup method
jokes.punchline: Claims bringing a cactus is a universal breakup signal
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic presented with complete confidence. Shows Michael's self-deception.

jokes.text: Well, if you ever change your mind, I ain't going nowhere.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound casual after being dumped
jokes.punchline: Uses awkward double negative that sounds needy
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's attempt at casual coolness failing completely. Good character work.

jokes.text: That's it, I'm out of here! Okay, I get it. Is that supposed to be me? No, that's my impression of Tobias doing you.
jokes.setup: Michael seeing GOB's impression
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's an impression of an impression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment that's both confusing and revealing about family dynamics. Layered perfectly.

jokes.text: Okay, I get it. Is that supposed to be me? No, that's my impression of Tobias doing you.
jokes.setup: Michael sees Tobias's impression
jokes.punchline: Tobias reveals it's an impression of an impression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy about performance and identity. Perfect Arrested Development recursive humor.

jokes.text: Why hasn't Michael come back? What happened to 'Michael always comes back'?
jokes.setup: Buster wondering about Michael's absence
jokes.punchline: References the show's central premise as family expectation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that works as both character moment and meta-commentary on show structure.
